title: The Opinion Of Men
content:
My desires are like the white snows on Fuji
    That grow but never melt.
    I am becoming proud of my bad reputation;
    And the more men say,
    We cannot understand why she loves him,
    The less I care.
    I am sure that in a very short time
    I shall give myself to him.

    Japanese Street Song.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edward Powys Mathers (As Translator), titled "The Opinion Of Men", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
